Dan Ives reveals his top AI picks and explains why he thinks Palantir is heading to a trillion-dollar valuation. Get the latest tech stock insights and strategies for navigating market volatility.

#AI #Investing #StockMarket #Palantir #DanIves #TechStocks https://t.co/4B3MKAMQ3y

Is Palantir the next Nvidia? Wedbush expert Dan Ives explains why he sees PLTR hitting a trillion-dollar valuation soon and breaks down the next AI stock surge. #Palantir #PLTR #AI #StockMarket #Investing https://t.co/FoKJsm2vJ1

My biggest problem with them is they own gadgets, but lack important foundation capabilities like a top battle tested C2/C4 system or deep tech stuff like lasers. ISR capabilities are third party software from Palantir. Understand the vision, but dilution is massive in relation to what is executed so far.
I need to make a 2x at minimum before end of 2027 to be interested. Palantir in my view is not going to be a 1T company in that timeframe.

The SaaSpocalypse narrative is starting to make less and less sense.

Software companies like $PLTR, $NOW, and $CRM are getting punished because investors believe AI will replace SaaS.

Jensen Huang disagrees.

“Claude can write software.”

That’s true.

But Jensen argues that enterprises aren’t going to build their own software.

They’re going to deploy AI agents that use software tools to get work done.

“Agents won’t replace tools. Agents will use tools.”

NVIDIA is already seeing this happen.

According to Jensen, the company has deployed so many AI agents that tool usage is actually incre

The "SaaSpocalypse" is creating some of the best opportunities in the market:

$CRM -60%
$NOW -60%
$PLTR -40%

The market believes LLMs will allow every company to build its own software.

Technically possible.

Economically irrational.

Building, maintaining, securing, and updating custom software at scale is far more expensive than most investors realize.

The reality is that these companies are becoming more valuable, not less.

They are the enablers of the AI revolution, not its victims.

Just look at their free cash flow.
All the $PLTR trolls gotta be fans because they keep on making new X accounts to comment under my comments. Cant do it like me son no matter how many times you try…

The @PalantirTech retail pumpers were writing about my achievements in 2021 in their @Substack releases that no one reads or subscribes to 

PT on $PLTR remains $2🔻regardless of the analyst desks banging on this name with a buy rating 

[I am an independent Wall Street analyst] from West California//

C O N T R A \ L S research
I’d pay 38x revs for $PLTR before I’d pay 32x revs for $CRWD and/or $NET 😂

$PLTR did $0.75 of EPS last year. 

Management just said they’ll do at least $1.50 of EPS this year. 

If you go back 3 years… the sell side analysts were looking for $PLTR to do $0.40 of EPS in 2026 and $0.50 of EPS in 2027. 

Obviously the analysts weren’t even close. It’s very possible their 55% EPS CAGR for next 3 years might also be too low. 

If they end up doing 65% CAGR over next 3 years then the stock is currently trading with a 1.3x PEG ratio on a 3 year EPS number which is much lower than the average Nasdaq 100 stock which is trading above a 2.0x PEG ratio on a 3 year EPS number.
